part.text:
So the current state: `src/mbgl/layout/symbol_instance.hpp` contains an inline shim that extends `namespace std` — which is undefined behavior. The task: create a proper core utility header (e.g., `include/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp` or `src/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp`) that:

- Uses `std::source_location` when available (via `__cpp_lib_source_location` / `__has_include(<source_location>)`)
- Otherwise provides `mbgl::util::source_location` fallback using `__builtin_FILE`, `__builtin_LINE`, `__builtin_FUNCTION` where supported, or `__FILE__`/`__LINE__` macros
- Does NOT extend `namespace std`
- Keeps call sites useful for diagnostics (i.e., SYM_GUARD_LOC still provides file/line/function)
- Update build manifests (CMakeLists.txt, BUILD.bazel for core headers)
- Commit the change

Let me look at how bucket.hpp and symbol_bucket use std::source_location, and check build manifests. Also check the other call sites: symbol_instance.cpp, symbol_bucket.cpp, bucket.hpp.

Let me check the existing util headers to match style, and the build files.

Wait: `__builtin_FILE` etc. as default args of a static member function — default args are evaluated at call site, so `current()` captures the caller. But with the macro `MLN_SOURCE_LOCATION_CURRENT()` expanding to `::mbgl::util::source_location::current()` at the actual call site, the default args capture that location. Good.

One issue: in the std path, `std::source_location` requires the include happened — if `__cpp_lib_source_location` is defined, `<source_location>` was included via __has_include. But some old compilers might define `__cpp_lib_source_location` without `__has_include` support... edge case, fine.

Also `#if MLN_UTIL_HAS_LOCATION_BUILTINS` — if not defined, `#if` treats undefined as 0. Fine.

The compile command has `-isystem` vendor includes; add `-I/tmp/opencode/slstub` — but wait, vendor includes use angle brackets and -isystem paths come after -I in search order, so stub `source_location` would shadow any vendor `#include <source_location>` too. Boost includes source_location... boost/assert/source_location.hpp includes <source_location> if std lib has it — it checks `__cpp_lib_source_location` which won't be defined with the stub, so boost uses its own. Good, that actually simulates OpenHarmony faithfully.

But -I/tmp/opencode/slstub comes before system libc++ includes — libc++ headers themselves may #include <source_location> internally (e.g., <string> in newer libc++... on this system it's libstdc++ probably since g++ worked; clang might use libstdc++ too). libstdc++ doesn't include source_location internally except where needed. part.text:
Done. Committed as `02315f54a7`.

## What changed

**New header: `include/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp`**
- When the standard library provides it (`__cpp_lib_source_location >= 201907L`), `mbgl::util::source_location` is simply an alias for `std::source_location` — the normal path is fully supported.
- Otherwise (e.g. OpenHarmony), a compatible fallback class is defined **in `mbgl::util`, never in `namespace std`**, with the same interface (`line()`, `column()`, `file_name()`, `function_name()`).
- Capture is done via `MLN_SOURCE_LOCATION_CURRENT()`, which works on all paths:
  - std path → `source_location::current()`
  - fallback with compiler intrinsics (`__builtin_FILE`/`__builtin_LINE`/`__builtin_FUNCTION`, detected via `__has_builtin` or GCC) → `current()` with call-site default arguments
  - last resort → plain standard `__FILE__`/`__func__`/`__LINE__`

**Call sites** (`symbol_instance.{hpp,cpp}`, `bucket.hpp`, `symbol_bucket.{hpp,cpp}`)
- Removed the UB shim that injected `std::source_location` into `namespace std`.
- Guard-check signatures now take `util::source_location`, and `SYM_GUARD_LOC` expands to `MLN_SOURCE_LOCATION_CURRENT()`, so crash diagnostics still log `function (file:line)` as before.

**Build manifests**
- Header added to `CMakeLists.txt` (`INCLUDE_FILES`) and `bazel/core.bzl` (`MLN_CORE_HEADERS`).

## Verification
- All 7 affected translation units compile with `-Werror` under the existing build flags — both with the real `<source_location>` and with a stubbed-out header simulating OpenHarmony's libc++ (14/14 configurations pass).
- Standalone runtime tests confirmed correct file/line/function capture on all three fallback variants with both clang++ and g++.
- `clang-format` reports no new violations (the one remaining warning is pre-existing).
